description: Schema: workflow definition accepts a one-time schedule trigger variant
Schema: workflow definition accepts a recurring schedule trigger variant
Schema: workflow definition without a trigger remains valid and is not coerced into an explicit manual trigger type
Server action: create workflow accepts one-time schedule trigger payloads
Server action: update workflow accepts recurring schedule trigger payloads
Guard: time-triggered workflow publish is rejected when EE-only scheduling support is unavailable
Contract: time-triggered workflow publish resolves the fixed synthetic payload schema ref
Contract: time-trigger fire payload matches the registered synthetic schema
Validation: publish rejects inferred payload schema mode for one-time schedule triggers
Validation: publish rejects inferred payload schema mode for recurring schedule triggers
Validation: one-time schedule publish rejects missing runAt timestamp
Validation: one-time schedule publish rejects invalid timestamp values
Validation: recurring schedule publish rejects cron expressions with six fields or seconds
Validation: recurring schedule publish rejects invalid timezone values
Validation: recurring schedule publish accepts valid 5-field cron expressions
List API: workflows with one-time schedule triggers are reported with a real schedule trigger type rather than heuristic event matching
List API: workflows with recurring schedule triggers are reported with a real recurring trigger type rather than heuristic event matching
Designer: user can select One-time schedule trigger and save a draft definition
Designer: user can select Recurring schedule trigger and save a draft definition
Designer: time-trigger selection hides event catalog and trigger mapping controls
Designer: time-trigger selection shows fixed clock payload schema preview
DB integration: publishing a one-time scheduled workflow creates a workflow schedule state row
DB integration: publishing a recurring scheduled workflow creates a workflow schedule state row
DB integration: workflow schedule state row persists runner job identifiers after registration
Launcher: event-triggered workflow starts still flow through the shared launcher after refactor
Launcher: one-time schedule job handler starts a workflow run through the shared launcher
Launcher: recurring schedule job handler starts a workflow run through the shared launcher
Publish lifecycle: publishing a one-time scheduled workflow registers a one-time job via scheduleJobAt
Publish lifecycle: publishing a recurring scheduled workflow registers a recurring job via scheduleRecurringJob
Lifecycle: pausing a published time-triggered workflow cancels or disables its registered schedule
Lifecycle: deleting a published time-triggered workflow cancels its registered schedule
Lifecycle: changing a published workflow from time trigger to event trigger removes its registered schedule
Reschedule: updating a published one-time trigger atomically cancels the old registration and stores the replacement registration
Reschedule: updating a published recurring trigger atomically cancels the old registration and stores the replacement registration
Fire semantics: one-time schedule fire starts exactly one workflow run
Fire semantics: recurring schedule fire starts exactly one workflow run for that fire
State: one-time workflow schedule is marked completed after a successful fire
State: completed one-time schedule does not refire on later worker ticks
Run provenance: one-time triggered run persists time-trigger provenance fields
Run provenance: recurring triggered run persists time-trigger provenance fields
UI: workflow runs list displays One-time schedule label for one-time-triggered workflows
UI: workflow runs list displays Recurring schedule label for recurring-triggered workflows
Idempotency: duplicate delivery of the same one-time schedule fire does not create duplicate runs
Idempotency: duplicate delivery of the same recurring schedule fire does not create duplicate runs
Regression: no-trigger workflows still publish and run correctly after time-trigger support is added
Regression: event-triggered workflows still publish and start correctly after launcher extraction
